name: hashboard-cli

metadata.name: hashboard-cli
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Command-line tools for interacting with Hashboard

documentation:
# hashboard-cli

[Command-line tools](https://docs.hashboard.com/docs/data-ops/cli) for interacting with [Hashboard](https://hashboard.com).

functions.name: hashboard.session.get_current_session
documentation:
Gets or creates a new session for the current CLI client.
It's helpful to have access to this globally for a few reasons:
1. Ensure that only one session ever gets created and used.
2. Let utilities (such as our analytics tracking logic) access
   the current session without having to plumb it everywhere.

functions.name: hashboard.filesystem.local_resources
documentation: Recursively searches root for files that represent Hashboard resources.

functions.name: hashboard.credentials.get_credentials
documentation: Returns the credentials to use, as specified by the user via environment variables or credentials filepath.

functions.name: hashboard.dbt_run_results_parser.parse_dbt_run_results
documentation: Parses a serialized JSON representation of dbt run results into a list of dbt models to rebuild

functions.name: hashboard.api.analytics.track
documentation:
Send an analytics event to the app server. If a session exists,
it'll be used to send this event so that the event is attributed
to the current user.

Args:
    event (str): Event name.
    value (dict): Dict including information about the event.
    duration (Optional[int]): Duration of the event in seconds. Defaults to None.

functions.name: hashboard.api.analytics.cli_with_tracking.set_raw_command
documentation: Setter for the global raw_command variable.

functions.name: hashboard.api.analytics.cli_with_tracking._parse_command
hashboard.api.analytics.cli_with_tracking._parse_param_names_to_display_names
documentation:
Click gives you param names as they're parsed into click variables.
e.g. For the `--credentials-filepath` option, they give you `credentials_filepath`
as the param name.
This is annoying for analytics where we want to see the option as they typed it.
So, we DFS the root context's `to_info_dict()` structure which _does_ have this
information to create a mapping from param name to displayed option name.

Args:
    info_dict (Optional[dict]): Current info dict to parse. Initial consumer should
      pass the root context.

Returns:
    dict: Dictionary mapping param name to displayed option name.

functions.name: hashboard.api.access_keys.existing_user.create_access_key
documentation:
Creates and saves a new access key for an existing user. Does not
require the user to have an existing saved access key to do authentication,
but rather opens a browser page that pushes them through our login flow.

Args:
    credentials_filepath (str): Filepath to save access key to.
    intended_project_id (Optional[str]): Project ID to create a new access key for.
      Required if the user belongs to multiple projects. Defaults to None.

functions.name: hashboard.api.access_keys.browser_auth.auth_and_handle_query_params
documentation:
Opens a browser page where the user can sign up or login at the specified
`base_url` with the given `query_params_dict` encoded. When signup or login
finishes, Hashboard's app server creates a new access key for the user (as
well as an account and project if the user is signing up).

To receive information back from this browser page about the newly created
access key (and potentially account and project), this function spins up a
local HTTP server that listens for GET requests. In addition to the
`query_params_dict` passed to this function, we also append a
`cli-auth-server-host` param specifying a redirect URL at the server that
this function spins up. Hashboard's app server makes a GET request to this URL
using query params to pass information about the created access key back to the
CLI client. `handle_get_query_params` is a callback that consumes the received
query params as a dictionary and returns a final redirect URL to send the user's
browser page to.

Args:
    handle_get_query_params (QueryParamsHandler): Callback that consumes query params dict and returns a redirect URL.
    base_url (str): Base URL to send the user to that should enable either signup or login.
    query_params_dict (dict): Dict of query params to append to `base_url`. This function internally handles adding
      the `cli-auth-server-host` param, so consumers need not worry about this.

classes.name: hashboard.api.access_keys.browser_auth.CallbackHandler

methods.name: hashboard.api.access_keys.browser_auth.CallbackHandler.do_GET
documentation:
Parses query params, hands them off to `handle_get_query_params`,
and redirects to the returned URL.

methods.name: hashboard.api.access_keys.browser_auth.CallbackHandler.log_message
documentation:
Disables logging in the CLI, which we don't want to expose to the user
unless in debug mode.

Class that handles requests to the local HTTP server that
`login_and_handle_query_params` spins up.

functions.name: hashboard.api.build.build_cli.build
documentation: Commands for managing Hashboard builds․ By default, creates a new build.

functions.name: hashboard.api.build.build_cli.build_create
documentation:
Creates and validates a new build.
If a dbt root was defined when initializing your Hashboard project will run
`dbt parse` to build Hashboard models defined in in dbt.
This is the default command if you just run `hb build`.

functions.name: hashboard.api.build.build_cli.build_apply
documentation: Applies the changes from a build to your project. If no build_id is provided, this command will apply the most recently created build in your session, if one exists.

functions.name: hashboard.api.build.dbt._get_compiled_db_info
documentation:
dbt supports a pattern called 'defer' for Slim CI style workflows and dbt Cloud (https://docs.getdbt.com/reference/node-selection/defer)
This means that dbt models can either point at a table in a prod target or a dev target however the manifest generated
by dbt commands always outputs the the tables as if they were created in the dev target. In order to figure out the current
state of the databases we compile an arbitrary dbt-SQL string that that returns a list of database_name.schema_name.table_name
references which reflect the state of the actual warehouse which is ensures we're using the same dbs and schemas that dbt is

functions.name: hashboard.api.build.dbt.handle_dbt_args
documentation:
Convenience wrapper for running `dbt parse` before `hb preview` or `hb deploy`.
Must be run from your Hashboard project directory.

classes.name: hashboard.api.build.build_status_display.BuildStatusDisplay
documentation: Helper to display a build's current information to the console.

functions.name: hashboard.api.login
documentation:
Authenticates the session with the provided credentials.

:return The user's project ID, if successfully logged in.
:raises ClickException if the login is not successful.

functions.name: hashboard.api.create_build_from_local_files
documentation: Creates a build using local files and returns the result.

functions.name: hashboard.api.clear_model_cache

functions.name: hashboard.api.pull_resource
documentation: Pulls the config for the given resource, or all resources in the project if none is specified.

functions.name: hashboard.api._parse_table_data
documentation: Formats table names for output, and returns tables names and schemas

functions.name: hashboard.api.get_tables

functions.name: hashboard.api.export_query

classes.name: hashboard.api.datasource.utils.DatabaseTypes
documentation: Make sure this stays consistent with glean/db/database_types.py

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.datasource
documentation: Commands for managing Hashboard data sources.

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.list_datasources
documentation:
See your available database connections.

A database connection can be added in the Settings tab on hashboard.com.

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.create

Commands for create are defined in datasource_management.py

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.update

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.create_bigquery
documentation:
Create a BigQuery data connection in your project. 
More info: https://docs.hashboard.com/docs/database-connections/bigquery

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.update_bigquery
documentation:
Update a BigQuery data connection in your project. 
The ID of the connection you provide must match the ID of an existing connection.
More info: https://docs.hashboard.com/docs/database-connections/bigquery

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.upload
documentation:
Upload file(s) to Hashboard that can be queried using duckdb and
used as the basis for a data model.

functions.name: hashboard.api.datasource.datasource_cli.test
documentation: Test a data connection by its name.

functions.name: hashboard.utils.dbt_properties._insert_hashboard_meta
documentation:
dbt_properties_raw_str
target_block: either model or column block
updated_meta: new hashboard meta to insert

functions.name: hashboard.utils.dbt_properties._insert_yaml_block
documentation:
Replace a YAML block in a string and return the modified string.

Args:
    yaml_content: The original YAML content as a string
    nested_key_path: The nested path to the key to replace (dot-separated)
    new_block: The new YAML block to insert
    replace_existing: If False, new_block will be inserted. If True, new_block will replace the existing block at this line.

Returns:
    A tuple containing:
    - The modified YAML content as a string
    - A boolean indicating whether the replacement was successful

Raises:
    ValueError: If the nested key is not found in the YAML content

classes.name: hashboard.utils.resource.Resource
documentation: Represents any Hashboard resource which can be backed by a file.
Right now, it is possible to represent invalid resources using this class since config types are only in TypeScript.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.cli
documentation: A command-line interface for interacting with Hashboard.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.init
documentation: Initializes the root of your Hashboard project.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.token
documentation:
Log into a Hashboard account and create a new access key.

If `--credentials-filepath` is passed, will save the access key in that location.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.tables
documentation: Lists the tables inside of a Hashboard data source.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.cache
documentation: Commands for managing Hashboard's data cache.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.cache_clear
documentation: Clears the cache associated with a Hashboard resource.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.pull
documentation:
Pulls the latest resource configuration from Hashboard into the working directory.

GRN is the Hashboard resource name of the target resource. If the resource has dependencies, they will also be
pulled. If no GRN is specified, all code-controlled resources in the project will be pulled.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.remove_from_code_control
documentation: Removes a Hashboard resource from code control.

functions.name: hashboard.cli.preview
documentation: Legacy (v2) command stubs #

functions.name: hashboard.cli._echo_table_not_found
documentation: If table is not found in the available tables, output warning and display available tables.

functions.name: hashboard.cli._echo_datasource_not_found
documentation: If datasource not found, output warning and available datasources.

variables.name: hashboard.utils.hbproject.MAX_DEFAULT_BUILD_SECS
documentation: One hour

functions.name: hashboard.utils.pull.get_local_file_mappings
documentation:
Given a project id and a mapping of current aliases -> grns returns a list of tuples with
the generated grn, file path, and resource config for local Hashboard files

functions.name: hashboard.utils.pull.optionally_update_dbt_properties_file
documentation: Returns touched file path if resource was updated, else None
